title: Noor and the Classroom Bunny
synopsis: Noor feels scared on her first day at preschool until she meets Cocoa, the classroom bunny, and discovers that being gentle and kind helps her make new friends and feel brave.

pages.text: Noor held her mama's hand tight. Today was her first day at preschool! She felt scared looking at the big red building with yellow windows. 'What if nobody likes me?' she wondered.

illustration.description: A young South Asian girl with dark braided hair wearing a bright purple backpack stands next to her mother outside a cheerful red brick preschool building. Noor looks worried, clutching her mother's hand, while other diverse children and parents walk toward the entrance. The building has yellow-framed windows and colorful flowers in front.
illustration.alt: Noor holding her mother's hand outside preschool, looking nervous

pages.text: Inside the classroom, Noor saw lots of children playing. There were blocks and books and art supplies everywhere. But Noor still felt scared. She hid behind her mama's long blue dress.

illustration.description: Interior of a bright, colorful preschool classroom with diverse children playing at different activity stations. Noor peeks out from behind her mother's blue dress, looking anxious. The room has rainbow decorations, low tables, and educational posters on the walls with warm yellow lighting.
illustration.alt: Noor hiding behind her mother in the busy classroom

pages.text: Ms. Chen, the teacher, smiled at Noor. 'Would you like to meet someone special?' she asked kindly. Noor felt a little curious but still scared. She nodded her head just a tiny bit.

illustration.description: A friendly Asian teacher with short black hair and a bright green cardigan kneels down to Noor's eye level, smiling warmly. Noor looks uncertain but interested, still close to her mother. The background shows colorful classroom elements slightly blurred, focusing on the gentle interaction between teacher and child.
illustration.alt: Ms. Chen kneeling down to talk to shy Noor

pages.text: Ms. Chen led Noor to a special corner. There was a cozy cage with soft blankets. Inside sat the fluffiest brown bunny Noor had ever seen! 'This is Cocoa, our classroom bunny,' Ms. Chen said.

illustration.description: A corner of the classroom with a large, clean rabbit habitat featuring a adorable chocolate-brown bunny sitting on soft blue blankets. Noor's eyes widen with wonder and delight as she discovers Cocoa. The corner has child-height viewing windows and educational posters about rabbit care with warm, inviting lighting.
illustration.alt: Noor discovering Cocoa the bunny in the classroom corner

pages.text: Cocoa had the softest brown fur and the sweetest pink nose. When she saw Noor, Cocoa hopped closer to the cage door. Noor felt happy for the first time today! 'She's so pretty!' Noor whispered.

illustration.description: Close-up view of Cocoa the bunny with rich chocolate-brown fur, bright dark eyes, and a pink nose, sitting near the cage entrance. Noor's face shows pure joy and wonder as she looks at the bunny. The illustration captures the magical moment of connection between child and animal with soft, warm colors.
illustration.alt: Noor smiling with joy while looking at Cocoa up close

pages.text: 'Would you like to pet Cocoa?' Ms. Chen asked. 'We must be very gentle with animals.' Noor felt brave now. She carefully put her small hand through the cage and touched Cocoa's soft fur.

illustration.description: Noor gently reaching her small hand into the rabbit habitat to pet Cocoa, with Ms. Chen supervising nearby. Noor's expression shows concentration and growing confidence. Cocoa appears calm and friendly, allowing the gentle touch. The scene emphasizes the tender interaction between child and animal.
illustration.alt: Noor carefully petting Cocoa with gentle hands

pages.text: Other children came to see what Noor was doing. Marcus and Lily wanted to meet Cocoa too. 'She's so soft!' said Lily. 'Can you show us how to pet her?' asked Marcus. Noor felt proud and happy.

illustration.description: Three diverse children gathered around Cocoa's habitat - Noor (South Asian), Marcus (African American boy with curly hair), and Lily (blonde girl with freckles). They're all looking at Cocoa with excitement and wonder. Noor appears confident now, ready to share her new knowledge about the bunny.
illustration.alt: Noor with new friends Marcus and Lily around Cocoa's cage

pages.text: 'Remember to be gentle,' Noor told her new friends, just like Ms. Chen had taught her. The children took turns petting Cocoa carefully. Cocoa seemed happy too - her nose twitched with joy!

illustration.description: All three children taking turns gently interacting with Cocoa, who appears content and relaxed. Noor is shown in a teaching role, demonstrating kindness to animals. The bunny's nose is visibly twitching, and her ears are perked up happily. The scene radiates friendship and shared wonder.
illustration.alt: Children taking turns petting happy Cocoa the bunny

pages.text: At the end of the day, Noor didn't feel scared anymore. She felt brave and happy! 'Mama, I made friends with Cocoa and Marcus and Lily!' she said. 'I can't wait to come back tomorrow!'

illustration.description: Noor running excitedly toward her mother at the classroom door, with a huge smile and open arms. In the background, Marcus and Lily wave goodbye, and Cocoa can be seen in her habitat. The scene is filled with warm evening light, showing Noor's transformation from scared to confident and joyful.
illustration.alt: Happy Noor running to her mother at the end of the school day

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Noor feel at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: Noor felt scared on her first day of preschool because everything was new.

questions.question: What is the classroom bunny's name?
questions.explanation: The classroom bunny's name is Cocoa because of her chocolate-brown fur.

questions.question: What did Ms. Chen teach Noor about petting animals?
questions.explanation: Ms. Chen taught Noor to be gentle when petting Cocoa the bunny.

questions.question: How did Noor feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: By the end, Noor felt brave and happy because she made new friends and wasn't scared anymore.

pages.text: Noor felt _____ on her first day of school, but meeting Cocoa the bunny made her feel _____.
Ms. Chen taught the children to be _____ when petting Cocoa's soft _____ fur.
